title: Multiple Softening Q-vectors Driving a Cascade of CDW Phases in $\mathrm{1T-VSe}_{2}$
authors: Zheng-Hong Li, Yung-Ting Lee, Yu-Chan Tai, Cheng-Tien Chiang, Chien-Cheng Kuo, Meng-Kai Lin, Chun-Liang Lin, Hung-Chung Hsueh, Ming-Chiang Chung, Po-Tuan Chen, Chi-Cheng Lee
abstract: Charge density wave (CDW) formation in two-dimensional materials is governed by complex competing lattice instabilities that remain incompletely understood. Here, we investigate the structural evolution of monolayer $\mathrm{1T-VSe}_{2}$ using first-principles electronic and phonon calculations. The pristine phase exhibits several imaginary-frequency phonon modes associated with dominant instability wave vectors $\mathrm{Q}_{CDW}$, which generate the first-generation CDW phases. Subsequent phonon analyses reveal that several of these intermediate structures remain dynamically unstable and undergo further symmetry-lowering distortions into larger superstructures. Through iterative phonon-driven relaxations, we identify multiple transformation pathways that converge toward the same low-energy $2\sqrt{3}\times4$ CDW configuration. Although these pathways originate from distinct intermediate CDW states, they ultimately reach nearly degenerate energetically stable phases, demonstrating that different phonon-driven routes can lead to the same ground-state configuration. The results establish a unified phonon-driven cascade mechanism for hierarchical CDW formation in monolayer $\mathrm{1T-VSe}_{2}$ and provide a systematic framework for understanding competing ordered phases in low-dimensional quantum materials.
summary: This paper reveals that the complex variety of charge density wave phases in monolayer VSe2 is not a collection of isolated states, but a continuous cascade of structural transitions driven by phonon instabilities. By tracking how different unstable modes lead to the same stable ground state, the authors provide a systematic framework for understanding hierarchical phase formation in 2D materials.
main_problem: Understanding the complex, hierarchical structural evolution and the causal relationships between competing Charge Density Wave (CDW) phases in monolayer 1T-VSe2.
main_result: The study identifies a phonon-driven cascade mechanism where multiple unstable wave vectors drive sequential symmetry-lowering distortions that eventually converge toward a common, low-energy stable configuration.
method: First-principles Density Functional Theory (DFT) calculations using the OpenMX package combined with an iterative phonon-driven relaxation algorithm to identify subsequent instabilities.
model_or_system: Monolayer 1T-VSe2, a two-dimensional transition-metal dichalcogenide (TMD) undergoing structural phase transitions.
key_observables: Phonon dispersion/spectra, imaginary-frequency phonon modes, CDW ordering vectors (Q-vectors), and total energy changes.
important_parameters: Wave vectors QA, QB, and QC; supercell sizes; energy reduction per atom (meV/atom).
main_assumptions: The study relies on the accuracy of the GGA-PBE exchange-correlation functional and the finite-displacement method for phonon calculations.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the phonon-driven cascade including phonon dispersions and lattice distortions; Figure 2 displays the phonon dispersion of the pristine phase along high-symmetry paths.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of CDW hierarchy, describes the DFT and iterative phonon analysis methodology, presents the identification of first-generation and second-generation CDW phases, demonstrates the convergence of different pathways to a common ground state, and concludes with a unified framework for CDW formation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
